body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Classifier device architecture
body.intention: I intend to verify the Classifier device reduces orientation token burn and that palace.codebase nodes stay accurate without noise.
body.description:
**What changed:** Classifier rack device owns all classification; BuilderReport filed at ticket creation; palace.codebase.* nodes annotated nightly + delta on close; in_flight flags surface contention.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) palace.codebase nodes accumulate stale annotations for deleted/moved files — check annotator handles missing files. (2) classify() called at filing may slow down /ticket for small S tickets where the cost is not worth it — check if a size gate makes sense. (3) Builder report wrong-file suggestions actively mislead DS — precision/recall scores should be visible early. (4) LLM annotation pass costs money on first full build — confirm flat-rate inference is used.
**Signals to watch:** precision/recall scores in palace node metadata after first 10 tickets; cost_usd per ticket before/after; annotator error logs; Nanny nightly log for annotator step.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-26 or after 10 tickets have builder_report sections.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred/clear/partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Annotated with outcome and closed.
